cashout_refi_30yr.description: Estimated 30yr cash-out refinance rate (conventional spread over base 30yr fixed)

heloc_vs_prime_spread.description: How far above Prime the HELOC avg sits (for the 'Prime + X' label in the hero card)

heloc_vs_refi_gap_pt.description: Rate differential between HELOC and cash-out refi (for the 'gap' figure in the hero card)

sample_heloc_interest_monthly.description: Monthly interest-only payment on a $100K HELOC at heloc_avg rate

sample_existing_payment.description: Illustrative existing mortgage P&I — $300K at 4.0% for 30yr. STATIC (illustrates the 'most homeowners have a low rate' scenario).

sample_heloc_total_monthly.description: HELOC path total monthly = existing + HELOC interest

sample_refi_monthly.description: Cash-out refi monthly P&I on $400K at cashout_refi_30yr for 30yr

sample_gap_dollars.description: Monthly cost difference: refi minus HELOC total

expanded.scope: manifest + grep articles for literal old-rate references and report

full.scope: manifest + flag full content audit; open a todo-list PR, do not auto-push article prose edits

locations.description: 30yr Fixed in the masthead rate bar
HELOC avg in the masthead rate bar
Prime rate in the masthead rate bar
15yr Fixed in the masthead rate bar
'Freddie Mac PMMS · Apr X, YYYY' label at the end of the masthead rate bar
'APR 9, 2026 rates · SAMPLE SCENARIO' kicker above the rate card on the homepage
'+ HELOC interest at X%: $Y' line on the hero card
locations.note: Update both the rate label and the dollar amount.

locations.description: 'New monthly: $X' total line on the HELOC side of the hero card
'$400K at X%: $Y' refi line on the hero card
'Refi costs $X/mo more' verdict line on the hero card

out_of_scope_for_normal_runs:
Any rate reference inside article prose (inside

tags in any file)
heloc-rates-2026.html body content -- this is the current-rates article and has many rate references; needs separate content strategy
Any dollar-math example inside an article body (e.g., 'On a $60,000 balance at 7.02%, that's about $357/mo')
Sources & further reading sections (URLs shouldn't drift with rates)
Article datePublished (this is the original publish date; don't touch)
always_update_after_changes: Bump dateModified in every Article schema where ANY rate-dependent value on that page changed
Bump sitemap.xml <lastmod> for every URL whose page content changed to today's date
